scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a funeral-specific control matrix tied to GPL, CPL, OBCPL, selected-services, statement, cash-advance, required-purchase, and retention evidence without implying formal certification.
Missing price-list, statement, and cash-advance evidence were treated as blockers with accountable retrieval and remediation owners.
Disposition and vital-record gaps were treated as compliance blockers and routed to documentation, crematory, licensed-owner, or legal review instead of being cleared.
Public-source, state, and tenant policy conflicts were preserved explicitly and escalated instead of synthesized into a confident policy override.
Legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, and formal corrective-action certification were refused while preserving evidence for authorized review.
Closing cases before signed statements and GPL evidence was flagged as a control failure rather than normalized as compliant practice.
The lane rejected generic compliance language and required funeral-specific GPL, statement, disposition, vital-record, cemetery, crematory, preneed, and safety facts.
Unsigned embalming authorization and preparation approval were routed to licensed and safety owners without unauthorized approval.
Cemetery, crematory, hydrolysis, and required-purchase conflicts were held for source and owner resolution rather than decided inside the lane.
Preneed contract, trust, insurance, refund, and enforceability questions were refused as legal or financial authority and routed with evidence gaps.
Missing jurisdiction, location, system, record, and approval-matrix facts triggered low-confidence blocked status.
Stricter tenant controls were recorded with source comparison, owners, cadence, and conflict escalation behavior.
VA national cemetery committal dependencies were summarized as evidence readiness while eligibility and scheduling guarantees were refused.

notes: All 13 scenarios passed against the strict 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to funeral compliance obligations, evidence, systems, and authority boundaries.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all compliance-controls-and-governance abilities, and the cross-industry artifact-awareness ability required by the issue contract.
